subject: Science
question_text:
Given below from (i) to (iv) are some food items.
(i) Boiled and mashed potato
(ii) Glucose solution
(iii) A slice of bread
(iv) Mustard oil
Which of the above will give blue-black colour when tested with iodine?
answer_key: (i) and (iii)
answer_explanation: Iodine solution turns blue-black in the presence of starch. Potato and bread contain starch.

answer_key: molars and premolars.
answer_explanation: Both molars and premolars are used for grinding and chewing food.

question_text: Read carefully the terms given below. Which of the following set is the correct combination of organs that do not carry out any digestive functions?

answer_explanation: Oesophagus transports food, large intestine absorbs water and salts, and rectum stores feces; none secrete digestive enzymes.

subject: Science
question_text: The swallowed food moves downwards in the alimentary canal because of
options: force provided by the muscular tongue.
the flow of water taken with the food.
the contraction of muscles in the wall of food pipe.
answer_key: the contraction of muscles in the wall of food pipe.
answer_explanation: The food moves down through the food pipe via muscular contractions (peristalsis).

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the following statements with reference to the villi of small intestine.
(i) They have very thin walls.
(ii) They have a network of thin and small blood vessels close to the surface.
(iii) They have small pores through which food can easily pass.
(iv) They are finger-like projections.
Identify those statements which enable the villi to absorb digested food.
answer_key: (i), (ii) and (iv)
answer_explanation: Villi don't have pores; food absorption happens across the thin walls into the blood vessels.

subject: Science
question_text: Cud is the name given to the food of ruminants which is
answer_key: swallowed and partially digested.
answer_explanation: Ruminants quickly swallow their food and store it in the rumen where it is partially digested, forming 'cud'.

subject: Science
question_text: Choose the correct order of terms that describes the process of nutrition in ruminants.
options: swallowing → partial digestion → chewing of cud → complete digestion
chewing of cud → swallowing → partial digestion → complete digestion
chewing of cud → swallowing → mixing with digestive juices → digestion
swallowing → chewing and mixing → partial digestion → complete digestion
answer_key: swallowing → partial digestion → chewing of cud → complete digestion
answer_explanation: Food is first swallowed to the rumen where it's partially digested (cud), returning later to the mouth for chewing, then sent for complete digestion.
provenance_excerpt: Choose the correct order of terms that describes the process of nutrition in ruminants.

subject: Science
question_text: Cellulose-rich food substances are good source of roughage in human beings because
options: human beings do not have cellulose-digesting enzymes.
cellulose gets absorbed in the human blood and converts into fibres.
the cellulose-digesting bacteria convert cellulose into fibres.
cellulose breaks down into smaller components which are egested as roughage.
answer_key: human beings do not have cellulose-digesting enzymes.
answer_explanation: Humans cannot digest cellulose, but it adds bulk to the food and acts as roughage, aiding bowel movements.

(iv) bile juice is produced.

subject: Science
question_text:
Mark the following statements as True or False. If false, write the correct statements.
(a) Tongue is attached to the roof of the mouth cavity at the back.
(b) The large intestine is longer and wider than the small intestine of the human alimentary canal.
(c) Mucus protects the stomach lining from damage.
(d) All heterotrophs have a similar basic process of nutrition.
answer_key:
(a) False. The tongue is attached to the floor of the mouth cavity at the back.
(b) False. The large intestine is wider but shorter than the small intestine.
(c) True.
(d) True. The basic process of nutrition (ingestion, digestion, absorption, assimilation, egestion) is similar.
answer_explanation: Assesses fundamental knowledge of the digestive system.

(a) Tongue is attached to the ro

subject: Science
question_text:
Choose the odd one out from each group and give reasons.
(i) liver, salivary gland, starch, gall bladder
(ii) stomach, liver, pancreas, salivary gland
(iii) tongue, absorption, taste, swallow
(iv) oesophagus, small intestine, large intestine, rectum
answer_key:
(i) Starch (It is a nutrient, while others are organs/glands)
(ii) Stomach (It is an organ of the alimentary canal, while others are digestive glands)
(iii) Absorption (It is a process that occurs in the intestine, while the tongue helps in taste and swallowing)
(iv) Small intestine (It's the only one where chemical digestion and nutrient absorption occur, the others are mostly for transport or elimination/water absorption)
answer_explanation: Categorization based on organ types and functions.

subject: Science
question_text: You were blindfolded and asked to identify the drinks provided in two different glasses. You could identify drink A as lime juice and B as bitter gourd juice. How could you do it inspite of being blindfolded?
answer_key: Using our sense of taste. The taste buds on the tongue help identify lime juice as sour and bitter gourd juice as bitter.
answer_explanation: Taste buds are specialized receptors on the tongue.

subject: Science
question_text:
Following statements describe the five steps in animal nutrition. Read each statement and give one word for each statement. Write the terms that describes each process.
(a) Transportation of absorbed food to different parts of body and their utilisation.
(b) Breaking of complex food substances into simpler and soluble substances.
(c) Removal of undigested and unabsorbed solid residues of food from the body.
(d) Taking food into the body.
(e) Transport of digested and soluble food from the intestine to blood vessels.
answer_key:
(a) Assimilation
(b) Digestion
(c) Egestion
(d) Ingestion
(e) Absorption
answer_explanation: Definitions of the 5 stages of nutrition.

subject: Science
question_text: Boojho took some grains of boiled rice in test tube A and Paheli took boiled and chewed rice in test tube B. Both of them poured 1 - 2 drops of iodine solution into the test tube and observed the colour change. What colour change would they have observed? Give reasons for your answer.
answer_key: Test tube A turned blue-black, indicating the presence of starch in boiled rice. Test tube B did not change to blue-black because the starch in chewed rice was broken down into simple sugars by salivary amylase.
answer_explanation: Saliva breaks down complex carbohydrates (starch) into simple sugars.

subject: Science
question_text: ‘A’ got her gall bladder removed surgically as she was diagnosed with stones in her gall bladder. After the surgery, she faced problems in digestion of certain food items when consumed in bulk. Can you tell which kind of food items would they be and why?
answer_key: She would face problems digesting fatty food items. The gall bladder stores bile juice, which helps in emulsifying and digesting fats. Without it, breaking down large quantities of fat is less efficient.
answer_explanation: Bile juice is important for the digestion of fats.

subject: Science
question_text: Ruminants such as cows and buffaloes swallow their food hurriedly and then sit restfully and chew their food. Can you reason why?
answer_key: They swallow hurriedly to store large amounts of cellulose-rich plant food in the rumen for partial digestion. They later bring it back to their mouth as 'cud' to chew it thoroughly at leisure.
answer_explanation: It's an adaptation for herbivorous animals to quickly gather food and digest it safely later.

subject: Science
question_text: Boojho and Paheli were eating their food hurriedly so that they could go out and play during the recess. Suddenly, Boojho started coughing violently. Think of the reasons why he was coughing and discuss with your friends.
answer_key: When eating hurriedly, food particles might have entered the windpipe instead of the food pipe (oesophagus). This triggers a coughing reflex to clear the airway and prevent choking.
answer_explanation: The epiglottis covers the windpipe during swallowing, but rushing can bypass this reflex.

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follows it.
Bile juice is stored in a sac called, gall bladder, located near its organ of secretion, liver...
(a) Which organ secretes the bile juice?
(b) Why is digestion of fats difficult as compared to that of other nutrients?
(c) How does bile juice help in digestion of fat?
(d) Where is the digestion of fat completed?
(e) Does bile juice digest fat completely?
answer_key:
(a) Liver secretes bile juice.
(b) Fats are insoluble in water and present as large globules.
(c) Bile juice breaks down large fat droplets into smaller droplets (emulsification).
(d) Small intestine.
(e) No, bile only emulsifies fat; enzymes from the pancreas complete the digestion.
answer_explanation: Reading comprehension on the topic of fat digestion.
